reasoning: The Lean statement captures exactly the content of Rudin's exercise 4.24: continuity on the open interval (ContinuousOn on Set.Ioo a b), the midpoint-inequality hypothesis quantified over all points in (a,b), and the conclusion ConvexOn ℝ (Set.Ioo a b) f (whose Mathlib definition gives the standard convex-combination inequality for all x,y in the set and nonnegative weights summing to 1). The only additional hypothesis `hab : a < b` merely makes explicit the standard convention that "(a,b)" denotes a nonempty open interval; when a ≥ b both the informal and Lean statements are vacuously true (Ioo a b = ∅, and ConvexOn ℝ ∅ f trivially holds), so the two statements are logically equivalent. This matches the worked example in the instructions where surfacing an implicit premise is judged faithful.
